content:
[EXAMPLE - quiz | en | General Knowledge]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"quiz","title":"Media Literacy Basics","difficulty_percentage":45,"questions":[{"question":"Which factor helps evaluate whether information is reliable?","options":["Only the color of the page","Source, evidence, date, and context","Whether it is very short","Whether it has no author"],"correct_index":1,"explanation":"Reliable evaluation considers source, evidence, date, context, purpose, and possible bias."},{"question":"What is the difference between a fact and an opinion?","options":["A fact can be checked with evidence; an opinion expresses a view","An opinion is always longer","A fact never needs context","A fact is always written online"],"correct_index":0,"explanation":"Facts can be verified with evidence, while opinions express judgments or preferences."},{"question":"Why should readers check the date of a source?","options":["Dates always prove truth","Some information changes over time","Older sources are never useful","Newer sources never contain errors"],"correct_index":1,"explanation":"Dates help judge whether information is current enough for the question being asked."}]}
```

[EXAMPLE - true_false | en | Entertainment: Books]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"true_false","title":"Poetry and Literature True or False","difficulty_percentage":40,"statements":[{"statement":"A simile usually compares using words such as like or as.","is_true":true,"explanation":"Similes commonly use like or as to make comparisons."},{"statement":"The speaker of a poem is always the same person as the poet.","is_true":false,"explanation":"The speaker is the voice in the poem and may be distinct from the poet."},{"statement":"Free verse poetry can avoid a strict meter or rhyme scheme.","is_true":true,"explanation":"Free verse does not require a fixed meter or rhyme pattern."},{"statement":"Theme and plot mean exactly the same thing.","is_true":false,"explanation":"Plot is the sequence of events; theme is a broader idea explored by the work."}]}
```

[EXAMPLE - word-pass | en | Entertainment: Film]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"word-pass","title":"Film Production Word Pass","difficulty_percentage":45,"words":[{"letter":"S","hint":"Written plan for dialogue, action, and scenes.","answer":"Script","starts_with":true},{"letter":"E","hint":"Process of arranging shots to shape rhythm and meaning.","answer":"Editing","starts_with":true},{"letter":"F","hint":"Recorded sound effect recreated in post-production.","answer":"Foley","starts_with":true},{"letter":"C","hint":"Craft of capturing moving images through camera and lighting choices.","answer":"Cinematography","starts_with":true},{"letter":"D","hint":"Stage that brings the finished film to audiences.","answer":"Distribution","starts_with":true}]}
```

[EXAMPLE - quiz | en | Sports]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"quiz","title":"Sportsmanship and Fitness","difficulty_percentage":35,"questions":[{"question":"What does fair play mean in sport?","options":["Ignoring all rules","Competing ethically while respecting rules and people","Letting opponents win every time","Avoiding competition"],"correct_index":1,"explanation":"Fair play means competing within rules while respecting opponents, officials, teammates, and safety."},{"question":"Which fitness component is most directly linked to sustained activity over time?","options":["Cardiovascular endurance","Uniform color","Scorekeeping","Coin tosses"],"correct_index":0,"explanation":"Cardiovascular endurance supports prolonged activity by the heart, lungs, and circulatory system."},{"question":"Which action is an example of unsafe or unethical play?","options":["Following the rules","Respecting officials","Cheating to gain an advantage","Practicing technique"],"correct_index":2,"explanation":"Cheating violates fair play and undermines safe, ethical competition."}]}
```

[EXAMPLE - true_false | en | Art]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"true_false","title":"Art and Architecture True or False","difficulty_percentage":45,"statements":[{"statement":"Printmaking can create multiple impressions from a prepared surface.","is_true":true,"explanation":"Printmaking transfers ink from a matrix or prepared surface and can produce editions."},{"statement":"Gothic architecture is commonly associated with pointed arches and ribbed vaults.","is_true":true,"explanation":"Pointed arches and ribbed vaults are common Gothic architectural features."},{"statement":"Complementary colors are always identical colors.","is_true":false,"explanation":"Complementary colors are commonly placed opposite each other on a color wheel and create contrast."},{"statement":"Art conservation aims to slow damage and preserve evidence, not simply make works look new.","is_true":true,"explanation":"Conservation balances preservation, documentation, materials, and historical integrity."}]}
```
